findings.title: Fragile directory cleanup after spawnSync on Windows
findings.summary: Tests in next-step.test.ts and orchestrator.test.ts use direct rm() cleanup in afterEach, which is prone to EBUSY errors on Windows when preceding spawn calls briefly lock the directory.
evidence: packages/remediate-code/tests/next-step.test.ts:78 - `afterEach` uses standard `rm(TEST_DIR)` without retry logic, despite earlier tests calling spawnSync on the CLI.

packages/remediate-code/tests/phase-plan.test.ts:74 - A comment explicitly notes 'Windows can hold a handle on a directory briefly after spawnSync returns, causing EBUSY when rm() runs immediately in afterEach' and introduces `rmWithRetry` to fix it, but this isn't shared across other integration tests.

findings.title: Fragile asynchronous state assertions using double setImmediate
findings.summary: The mcp-server test suite uses a hardcoded double setImmediate delay to wait for asynchronous stream processing, which can lead to race conditions and flaky tests.
evidence: packages/remediate-code/tests/mcp-server.test.ts:15 - `flushAsync` waits exactly two ticks via `setImmediate` instead of awaiting a deterministic event or promise resolution from the MCP server.

findings.title: Missing edge-case test coverage for structural validation rules
findings.summary: The test suite lacks negative test cases for nested array element structures, such as verifying validation errors when affected_files contains malformed elements.
evidence: packages/remediate-code/tests/validation.test.ts:86 - Only tests that `affected_files` being entirely missing raises an error.

packages/remediate-code/src/validation/remediationState.ts:63 - The loop `for (const [i, file] of value.affected_files.entries()) { if (!isRecord(file) || typeof file.path !== 'string') ... }` is present in the source but left entirely untested.

findings.title: Missing tests for artifacts validations
findings.summary: There are no tests in the suite covering the complex validateArtifacts function.
evidence: packages/remediate-code/tests/validation.test.ts:2 - Imports focus entirely on `remediationState.js`, completely omitting tests for `artifacts.ts`.

packages/remediate-code/src/validation/artifacts.ts:287 - `validateArtifacts` is a critical 120+ line function responsible for verifying the entire remediation artifact directory structure, but it has no unit tests.
